Name: efficient_inference_optimization
Title: Optimizing Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs through Kernel-Level Throughput Engineering
Short Hypothesis: By applying kernel-level throughput engineering techniques, such as optimizing memory-bandwidth utilization and tensor-core occupancy, we can achieve significant speedups in language model inference on commodity GPUs without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works, such as Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding and CPU-GPU Workload Distribution, have explored optimizing language model inference on commodity GPUs. However, these approaches focus on high-level optimizations, such as model quantization and workload distribution, rather than kernel-level optimizations. Our proposal distinguishes from existing work by focusing on optimizing the underlying compute kernels and memory access patterns to achieve efficient inference.
Abstract: Large language models have achieved state-of-the-art results in various natural language processing tasks, but their computational requirements make them challenging to deploy on commodity hardware. To address this, we propose a kernel-level throughput engineering approach to optimize language model inference on commodity GPUs. Our approach focuses on optimizing memory-bandwidth utilization, tensor-core occupancy, and compute kernel efficiency to achieve significant speedups without sacrificing accuracy. We evaluate our approach on various language models and benchmark datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness in achieving efficient inference on commodity GPUs.
Experiments: Optimize memory-bandwidth utilization by rearranging memory access patterns and reducing memory allocation overhead
Improve tensor-core occupancy by optimizing compute kernel launch configurations and reducing synchronization overhead
Evaluate the effectiveness of our approach on various language models, including BERT, RoBERTa, and XLNet, and benchmark datasets, such as GLUE and SQuAD
Compare our approach to existing optimization techniques, such as model quantization and workload distribution, and evaluate its impact on accuracy and inference speed
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of optimizing kernel-level computations may require significant expertise and development time
The approach may not be effective for all language models or benchmark datasets, and may require model-specific or dataset-specific optimizations
The evaluation metrics used to measure the effectiveness of our approach may not capture all aspects of inference performance, such as energy efficiency or latency
